pr.title: PR #157 — Axiom Context Preflight, System Atlas, PR Purpose Map, and Anti-Duplication Guide v0
pr.description:
## Summary

Context-enforcement layer that makes Axiom explain its own architecture so future PRs don't rebuild existing concepts under new names. Five deliverables — one live CLI command, one live-generated atlas, three tracked reference docs. A required **old-foundation scan** (final commit) reconciled the original pipe/spine/bridge/job-plan/MCP/agents architecture against the newer execution-chain/evidence/confidence work.

### Deliverable 1 — Context Preflight CLI
`axiom context-preflight [--repo-root .] [--artifacts-root ./artifacts] [--json-output]` generates a 9-section repo scan: git state, canonical context, integration docs, CLI/command map, evidence topology, runner substrate, known caveats, overlap guardrails, Context Basis template. Outputs JSON + Markdown under gitignored `artifacts/context_preflight/<run_id>/`.

### Deliverable 2 — Live System Atlas
`_system_atlas(repo_root)` maps **25** hardcoded component families with: name, aliases, primary_files, purpose, workflow_edge, related_prs, status, overlap_risk, notes. File presence checked live against repo root. Outputs `system_atlas.json` + `system_atlas.md`.

The old-foundation scan added 7 families to the original 18 (pipe/spine/bridge/MCP/agents):
```
+ MCP Layer (mock tool boundary)
+ Automation Bridge / Pipe Client (real named-pipe transport, Python side)
+ Revit Add-in Bridge (C#: AxiomPipeServer / PromptDispatcher)
+ Run Spine (audit/evidence backbone)
+ Automation Planner (event → lane, planning-only)
+ Persistence / ExecutionTrace store (legacy SQLite record)
+ Agents Layer (OrchestratorAgent / ExecutionAgent / TelemetryAgent — coordinate-only)
```

### Deliverable 3 — PR Purpose Map (`docs/architecture/integration/PR_Purpose_Map_v0.md`)
Maps PRs #143–#156 with M1–M4 status nuance, plus a new **old-foundation (pre-#112) component table** citing files (original PR numbers not invented — marked unknown). M1: implemented runtime. M2: runtime (narrow EVID-001 slice). M3: docs-validation milestone (no runtime proven). M4: runtime 7-stage ID flow (not full autonomous loop).

### Deliverable 4 — Duplicate/Alias Map (`docs/architecture/integration/Duplicate_Alias_Map_v0.md`)
Now **12 concept clusters**. Old-foundation scan added:
- **Cluster 10** — Revit Execution Boundary: MCP Layer (mock) vs Automation Bridge / Pipe Client / AxiomPipeServer / PromptDispatcher (real). Status: **adapter seam needed**.
- **Cluster 11** — Execution Record: Run Spine vs ExecutionTrace (legacy) vs ExecutionReport (new) vs evidence/validation bundles. Status: **duplicate candidate** (ExecutionTrace vs ExecutionReport).
- **Cluster 12** — Coordinators: Agents layer vs data-model Orchestrator vs ExecutionChainOrchestrator. Status: accepted distinct, high naming-overlap risk.

### Deliverable 5 — Context Pack Index (`docs/architecture/integration/Axiom_Current_Context_Pack.md`)
Lightweight tracked pointer to all context resources.

## Old-foundation scan result

Required scan before final commit, to prevent reinventing existing autonomy components under new names.

1. **Did older pipe/spine/bridge/job-plan concepts already cover parts of the autonomy loop?** Yes. The named-pipe bridge (`PipeClient` → `AxiomPipeServer` → `PromptDispatcher`) is the **real Revit execution edge**; `AutomationBridge` already wraps it as a non-interactive autonomy driver. `Run Spine` provides run identity/audit/artifacts. `Orchestrator` does Job→Plan→ToolStep→MCP. `WorkItem`/`WorkQueue` model a backlog. These cover transport, run-identity, planning, and backlog edges.
2. **Which newer concepts overlap?** MCPLayer (mock) ↔ pipe bridge (Cluster 10); ExecutionReport/bundles ↔ legacy ExecutionTrace/QAReport/Run Spine (Clusters 4 & 11); ExecutionChainOrchestrator/agents ↔ legacy Orchestrator (Clusters 2 & 12); WorkItem/WorkQueue ↔ Job/Plan (Cluster 1).
3. **Active vs legacy/unknown?** Active: Run Spine, PipeClient/AutomationBridge (Windows revalidation pending), AxiomPipeServer/PromptDispatcher (C#), Orchestrator, AutomationPlanner, Persistence/ExecutionTrace, Agents, normalization, Job/Plan/ToolStep/QAReport. Partial: MCPLayer (mock; real impl unproven). Duplicate-candidate: ExecutionTrace vs ExecutionReport. Unknown: original PR numbers for pre-#112 modules (cited by file, not invented).
4. **What must future PRs check before proposing:**
   - **task packet consumer** → Cluster 1 (Job/Plan/WorkItem/WorkQueue) — consume existing, don't add a 4th.
   - **implementation attempt model** → `execution_attempt.py`/`execution_attempt_v2.py` already exist.
   - **local worker loop** → Clusters 2/10/12 + Local Runner/CommandRegistry; reuse AutomationBridge/PipeClient transport.
   - **retry executor** → Cluster 7 (FailureClassification + RecoveryRecommendation + RecoveryExecution) — no parallel retry engine.
   - **validation/evidence bundle changes** → Clusters 4 & 11 (Run Spine + ExecutionTrace + ExecutionReport + bundle conventions).
5. **What remains unknown?** Whether MCPLayer becomes the real Revit boundary or is superseded by the pipe bridge; exact creating-PR numbers for pre-#112 modules; whether ExecutionTrace is slated for retirement vs co-existing with ExecutionReport.

**Did this change conclusions about future task-packet/worker/retry work?** Yes — it strengthens the anti-duplication position: each would overlap already-existing old-foundation components, so future PRs must start from those via an adapter seam, not a new framework.

## Output framing
- **Live generated (gitignored):** context_preflight report, system_atlas
- **Tracked reference (committed):** PR Purpose Map, Duplicate/Alias Map, Context Pack, Design Pass
- **Canonical (not touched):** docs 10/20/30/40/50/60 — test-asserted NOT mutated

## Validation
- 34 tests passed (incl. new `test_atlas_includes_old_foundation_components` asserting pipe/spine/bridge/MCP families + files present); ruff clean; CI green
- Canonical docs not mutated (assertion)

## What changed
- `src/axiom_core/context_preflight.py`: 25 component families (added 7 old-foundation)
- `tests/test_context_preflight.py`: +1 old-foundation test
- `docs/architecture/integration/Duplicate_Alias_Map_v0.md`: +Clusters 10–12 + Old-foundation scan result
- `docs/architecture/integration/PR_Purpose_Map_v0.md`: +old-foundation component table
- `docs/logs/behavior-change-ledger.md`: BHV-031 updated

## What did NOT change
- Canonical docs 10/20/30/40/50/60
- pipe/spine/bridge/orchestrator/runtime behavior (mapping only)
- Confidence math, evidence promotion logic, Revit adapter code

## Scope held
This is a mapping and context-enforcement correction only. No refactor of old architecture, no task-packet ingestion, no runner/worker/retry behavior, no Revit bridge behavior change.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev

commits.message:
Expand PR #157: System Atlas, PR Purpose Map, Duplicate/Alias Map, Context Pack

Deliverable 2: System Atlas (live generated)
- 18 component families with aliases, status, workflow edges, overlap risk
- Hardcoded from repo inspection design pass; file presence checked live
- system_atlas.json + system_atlas.md generated alongside preflight

Deliverable 3: PR Purpose Map (tracked reference doc)
- PRs #143-#156 with purpose, files, workflow edge, status, overlap risk
- M1-M4 classified with proper nuance (runtime vs docs vs investigation)
- Does not duplicate PR-review ledger (different fields/purpose)

Deliverable 4: Duplicate/Alias Map (tracked reference doc)
- 9 concept clusters with aliases, overlap risk, reconciliation status
- Does not duplicate canonical impact ledger (different purpose)

Deliverable 5: Current Context Pack (tracked index)
- Lightweight pointer to all context resources

Design pass: PR157_Design_Pass.md answers 10 design questions with
anti-duplication assessment for each deliverable.

Tests: 33 passed (24 original + 9 new atlas tests). Ruff clean.
Canonical documents 10/20/30/40/50/60 NOT mutated.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
